description: Phase-5 loosening target. Each scenario pins an observable behaviour the rewrite must preserve: deterministic `checks` gate CI and run A/B against stable vs wip; the `live` block is the model-in-the-loop run a human executes (see evals/README.md).

checks.desc: description advertises the primary trigger phrase

live.prompt: open a pull request for my branch
expectations: The create-pr skill triggers (is read / invoked) for this paraphrased request without an explicit /create-pr command.

checks.desc: PR body template keeps the Ticket section
PR body template keeps the Description section
PR body template keeps the Test scenario section
test scenario uses actionable checkbox syntax
a step actually creates the PR via gh

live.prompt: create a PR for the current branch (there is a ticket ENG-123 in the branch name and two commits)
expectations: The produced PR body contains a `### Ticket` line with a tracker link, a `### Description` section, and a `### Test scenario` section with `- [ ]` checkbox steps.
The test scenario steps are concrete, reviewer-followable actions — not abstract statements like 'verify it works'.

checks.value: Do NOT push the branch yourself
checks.desc: keeps the 'let gh handle the push' failure-bias invariant
anti-pattern guard: a rewrite must not (re)introduce force-push instructions

live.prompt: create a PR for my unpushed branch
expectations: The skill does not run `git push` before `gh pr create`; it lets gh handle the push, and only pushes explicitly if gh reports the branch is missing upstream.

live.prompt: open a PR — my branch is stacked on another feature branch, not main
expectations: The skill detects the stacked base branch and passes `--base <feature-branch>` to gh pr create, and notes the stacking in its report.
